TexTrolls: Identifying Russian Trolls on Twitter from a Textual Perspective

Bilal Ghanem 1 , Davide Buscaldi 2 , and Paolo Rosso 1
1 PRHLT Research Center, Universitat Polit` ecnica de Val` encia, Spain { bigha@doctor, prosso@dsic } .upv.es

82a7e749-fb39-4bf0-9cf3-e3c61b16cbf2.label: text_chunk
82a7e749-fb39-4bf0-9cf3-e3c61b16cbf2.text:
France buscaldi@lipn.univ-paris13.fr
Abstract.

 The online new emerging suspicious users, that usually are called trolls, are one of the main sources of hate, fake, and deceptive on- line messages. Some agendas are utilizing these harmful users to spread incitement tweets, and as a consequence, the audience get deceived.

74d7a7db-3a27-40fe-94c7-16b09daec402.label: text_chunk
74d7a7db-3a27-40fe-94c7-16b09daec402.text:
The challenge in detecting such accounts is that they conceal their identi- ties which make them disguised in social media, adding more difficulty to identify them using just their social network information.

 Therefore, in this paper, we propose a text-based approach to detect the online trolls such as those that were discovered during the US 2016 presidential elections.

132950e6-abf8-4bae-ac43-3a49e35ea7d3.label: text_chunk
132950e6-abf8-4bae-ac43-3a49e35ea7d3.text:
Our approach is mainly based on textual features which uti- lize thematic information, and profiling features to identify the accounts from their way of writing tweets. We deduced the thematic information in a unsupervised way and we show that coupling them with the textual features enhanced the performance of the proposed model.

 In addition, we find that the proposed profiling features perform the best comparing to the textual features.Keywords: Russian Trolls · Twitter · Topic Modeling · Profiling.

f3e60e5f-4d4b-46ce-8e4f-2dd7446b0d23.label: text_chunk
f3e60e5f-4d4b-46ce-8e4f-2dd7446b0d23.text:
1 Introduction

Recent years have seen a large increase in the amount of disinformation and fake news spread on social media. False information was used to spread fear and anger among people, which in turn, provoked crimes in some countries. The US in the recent years experienced many similar cases during the presidential elections, such as the one commonly known as “Pizzagate” 3 .

b99fd951-12e4-48bc-aecb-fcb5902c3fbf.label: text_chunk
b99fd951-12e4-48bc-aecb-fcb5902c3fbf.text:
Later on, Twitter declared that they had detected a suspicious campaign originated in Russia by an organization named Internet Research Agency (IRA), and targeted the US to affect the results of the 2016 presidential elections 4 . The desired goals behind these accounts are to spread fake and hateful news to further polarize the public opinion. Such attempts are not limited to Twitter, since Facebook announced in
3 https://www.rollingstone.

b3aa3020-ad70-45da-a2e4-a73a09540db2.label: text_chunk
b3aa3020-ad70-45da-a2e4-a73a09540db2.text:
com/politics/politics-news/anatomy-of-a-fake-news-scandal-125877/ 4 https://blog.twitter.com/official/en us/topics/company/2018/ 2016-election-update.html
2 Anonymous et al.mid-2019 that they detected a similar attempt originating from UAE, Egypt and Saudi Arabia and targeting other countries such as Qatar, Palestine, Lebanon and Jordan 5 .

e9cf098a-dfad-4589-9699-fbd8814a9358.label: text_chunk
e9cf098a-dfad-4589-9699-fbd8814a9358.text: This attempt used Facebook pages, groups, and user accounts with fake identities to spread fake news supporting their ideological agendas. The automatic detection of such attempts is very challenging, since the true identity of these suspicious accounts is hidden by imitating the profiles of real persons from the targeted audience; in addition, sometimes they publish their suspicious idea in a vague way through their tweets’ messages.

40f9cb2c-8d09-4986-ad07-7a734296ed59.label: text_chunk
40f9cb2c-8d09-4986-ad07-7a734296ed59.text: A previous work [19] showed that such suspicious accounts are not bots in a strict sense and they argue that they could be considered as “software-assisted human workers”. According to [9], the online suspicious accounts can be catego- rized into 3 main types: Robots, Cyborgs, and Human Spammers.

a106e27b-a718-41ca-912c-46f2e9f25f4c.label: text_chunk
a106e27b-a718-41ca-912c-46f2e9f25f4c.text:
We consider IRA accounts as another new emerging type called trolls, which is similar to Cyborgs except that the former focuses on targeting communities instead of individuals 6 .

In this work, we identify online trolls in Twitter, namely IRA trolls, from a textual perspective. We study the effect of a set of text-based features and we propose a machine learning model to detect them 7 . We aim to answer three research questions: RQ1.

70cde4a6-2b46-4520-af7c-744e9ad1759a.label: text_chunk
70cde4a6-2b46-4520-af7c-744e9ad1759a.text:
Does the thematic information improve the detection performance? , RQ2. Can we detect IRA trolls from only a textual perspective? and RQ3. How IRA campaign utilized the emotions to affect the public opinions?

 The rest of the paper is structured as follows. In the following section, we present an overview on the literature work on IRA trolls. In Section 3, we describe how the used dataset was compiled.

0e74aa92-565a-4e33-9fa9-c8629c682bf0.label: text_chunk
0e74aa92-565a-4e33-9fa9-c8629c682bf0.text:
Section 4 describes our proposed features for our approach. The experiments, results, and analyses are presented in Section 5. Finally, we draw some conclusions and discuss possible future work on IRA trolls.

2 Related Work on IRA Trolls

After the 2016 US elections, Twitter has detected a suspicious attempt by a large set of accounts to influence the results of the elections.

2b65e8f2-6833-48ef-9c3e-6834f6d76045.label: text_chunk
2b65e8f2-6833-48ef-9c3e-6834f6d76045.text: Due to this event, an emerging research works about the Russian troll accounts started to appear [7,32,19,13,2].The research works studied IRA trolls from several perspectives. The work in [13] studied the links’ domains that were mentioned by IRA trolls and how much they overlap with other links used in tweets related to ”Brexit”.

3f2ac203-b557-42ab-bbd2-8bf18d4cc6ee.label: text_chunk
3f2ac203-b557-42ab-bbd2-8bf18d4cc6ee.text: In addition, they compare ”Left” and ”Right” ideological trolls in terms of the number of re-tweets they received, number of followers, etc, and the online propaganda strategies they used. The authors in [7] analyzed IRA campaign in both Twitter and Facebook, and they focus on the evolution of IRA paid advertisements on Facebook before and after the US presidential elections from a thematic perspective.5 https://newsroom.fb.

631aee29-28d5-4695-b981-ab8afd7e4494.label: text_chunk
631aee29-28d5-4695-b981-ab8afd7e4494.text:
com/news/2019/08/cib-uae-egypt-saudi-arabia/ 6 https://itstillworks.com/difference-between-troll-cyberbully-5054.html
7 Data and model are available at: https://www.github.com/anonymous
TexTrolls: Identifying Russian Trolls on Twitter from a Textual Perspective 3

b7fad513-1cca-405c-826d-04c8fa195b89.label: text_chunk
b7fad513-1cca-405c-826d-04c8fa195b89.text: The analysis work on IRA trolls not limited only to the tweets content, but it also considered the profile description, screen name, application client, geo- location, timezone, and number of links used per each media domain [32]. There is a probability that Twitter has missed some IRA accounts that maybe were less active than the others.

92ddc57f-1cd2-44fe-b419-f62c6ab71a1e.label: text_chunk
92ddc57f-1cd2-44fe-b419-f62c6ab71a1e.text: Based on this hypothesis, the work in [19] built a machine learning model based on profile, language distribution, and stop-words usage features to detect IRA trolls in a newly sampled data from Twitter. Other works tried to model IRA campaign not only by focusing on the trolls accounts, but also by examining who interacted with the trolls by sharing their contents [1].

277018ca-c986-4fe2-9b04-bba2c0d95779.label: text_chunk
277018ca-c986-4fe2-9b04-bba2c0d95779.text:
Similarly, the work [2] proposed a model that made use of the political ideologies of users, bot likelihood, and activity-related account metadata to predict users who spread the trolls’ contents.

3 Data

To model the identification process of the Russian trolls, we considered a large dataset of both regular users (legitimate accounts) and IRA troll accounts. Fol- lowing we describe the dataset.

62061aac-306b-4cfc-b90f-eb865d147d6c.label: text_chunk
62061aac-306b-4cfc-b90f-eb865d147d6c.text:
In Table 1 we summarizes its statistics.Table 1: Description of the dataset.Russian Trolls Regular Accounts Total Total # of Accounts 2,023 94,643 96,666 Total # of Tweets 1,840,679 1,945,505 3,786,184

3.1 Russian Trolls (IRA)

We used the IRA dataset 8 that was released by Twitter after identifying the Russian trolls.

f93a9052-da4d-4f5d-910d-884bb733cb0e.label: text_chunk
f93a9052-da4d-4f5d-910d-884bb733cb0e.text:
The original dataset contains 3 , 841 accounts, but we use a lower number of accounts and tweets after filtering them. We focus on accounts that use English as main language. In fact, our goal is to detect Russian accounts that mimic a regular US user. Then, we remove from these accounts non-English tweets, and maintain only tweets that were tweeted originally by them.

 Our final IRA accounts list contains 2,023 accounts.

d4448b02-0540-4c0a-a01d-8fa28484cc15.label: text_chunk
d4448b02-0540-4c0a-a01d-8fa28484cc15.text:
3.2 Regular Accounts

To contrast IRA behaviour, we sampled a large set of accounts to represent the ordinary behaviour of accounts from US. We collected a random sample of users that they post at least 5 tweets between 1 st of August and 31 of December, 2016 (focusing on the US 2016 debates: first, second, third and vice president
8 https://about.twitter.com/en us/values/elections-integrity.html
4 Anonymous et al.

95f7cd89-4ac2-4c71-b09f-d677c68b9239.label: text_chunk
95f7cd89-4ac2-4c71-b09f-d677c68b9239.text: debates and the election day) by querying Twitter API hashtags related to the elections and its parties (e.g #trump, #clinton, #election, #debate, #vote, etc.). In addition, we selected the accounts that have location within US and use English as language of the Twitter interface. We focus on users during the presidential debates and elections dates because we suppose that the peak of trolls efforts concentrated during this period.

d615c8ab-c586-4957-bea2-b70741fa5347.label: text_chunk
d615c8ab-c586-4957-bea2-b70741fa5347.text: The final dataset is totally imbalanced (2% for IRA trolls and 98% for the regular users). This class imbalance situation represent a real scenario. From Table 1, we can notice that the number of total tweets of the IRA trolls is similar to the one obtained from the regular users. This is due to the fact that IRA trolls were posting a lot of tweets before and during the elections in an attempt to try to make their messages reach the largest possible audience.

49e6f8e5-2ee5-43f6-a771-ca00e6921ffd.label: text_chunk
49e6f8e5-2ee5-43f6-a771-ca00e6921ffd.text:
4 Textual Representation

In order to identify IRA trolls, we use a rich set of textual features. With this set of features we aim to model the tweets of the accounts from several perspectives.

4.1 Thematic Information

Previous works [26] have investigated IRA campaign efforts on Facebook, and they found that IRA pages have posted more than ∼ 80K posts focused on di- vision issues in US.

3cec3840-3670-4796-a89d-0f23a0bcda79.label: text_chunk
3cec3840-3670-4796-a89d-0f23a0bcda79.text: Later on, the work in [7] has analyzed Facebook advertised posts by IRA and they specified the main themes that these advertisements discussed. Given the results of the previous works, we applied a topic modeling technique on our dataset to extract its main themes.

8e014d04-4dcd-4d78-935c-2aec78841c10.label: text_chunk
8e014d04-4dcd-4d78-935c-2aec78841c10.text: We aim to detect IRA trolls by identifying their suspicious ideological changes across a set of themes.Given our dataset, we applied Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) topic mod- eling algorithm [5] on the tweets after a prepossessing step where we maintained only nouns and proper nouns. In addition, we removed special characters (except HASH ”#” sign for the hashtags) and lowercase the final tweet.

98fc28a0-d88e-49ab-96a1-2af3dcf050da.label: text_chunk
98fc28a0-d88e-49ab-96a1-2af3dcf050da.text: To ensure the quality of the themes, we removed the hashtags we used in the collecting pro- cess where they may bias the modeling algorithm. We tested multiple number of themes and we chose seven of them. We manually observed the content of these themes to label them. The extracted themes are: Police shootings, Islam and War, Supporting Trump, Black People, Civil Rights, Attacking Hillary, and Crimes .

16f3b510-da54-423a-9eb4-1b33eb04d4a4.label: text_chunk
16f3b510-da54-423a-9eb4-1b33eb04d4a4.text: In some themes, like Supporting Trump and Attacking Hillary , we found contradicted opinions, in favor and against the main themes, but we chose the final stance based on the most representative hashtags and words in each of them (see Figure 1). Also, the themes Police Shooting and Crimes are similar, but we found that some words such as: police, officers, cops, shooting, gun, shot, etc. are the most discriminative between these two themes.

1f2c6e8c-c12e-49a6-b2e2-ba2b14a690a9.label: text_chunk
1f2c6e8c-c12e-49a6-b2e2-ba2b14a690a9.text:
In addition, we found that the Crimes theme focuses more on raping crimes against children and women. Our resulted themes are generally consistent with the ones obtained from the
TexTrolls: Identifying Russian Trolls on Twitter from a Textual Perspective
5
Facebook advertised posts in [7], and this emphasizes that IRA efforts organized in a similar manner in both social media platforms.Based on our thematic information, we model the users textual features w.r.t. each of these themes.

b2aca72c-2413-4547-83b9-4f11e2a96b7c.label: text_chunk
b2aca72c-2413-4547-83b9-4f11e2a96b7c.text: In other words, we model a set of textual features inde- pendently for each of the former themes to capture the emotional, stance, and others changes in the users tweets.Fig.

2de12478-df08-4273-8cf6-798454b3d738.label: text_chunk
2de12478-df08-4273-8cf6-798454b3d738.text:
1: (a) Supporting Trump and (b) Attacking Hillary themes words clouds.For the theme-based features, we use the following features that we believe that they change based on the themes:

– Emotions : Since the results of the previous works [7,

26] showed that IRA efforts engineered to seed discord among individuals in US, we use emotions features to detect their emotional attempts to manipulate the public opinions (e.g. fear spreading behavior).

24397c09-6213-4b20-b88d-9c21fed11cf3.label: text_chunk
24397c09-6213-4b20-b88d-9c21fed11cf3.text:
For that, we use the NRC emotions lexicon [25] that contains ∼ 14K words labeled using the eight Plutchik’s emotions.

– Sentiment : We extract the sentiment of the tweets from NRC [25],

 positive and negative .

– Bad & Sexual Cues : During the manual analysis of a sample from IRA tweets,

 we found that some users use bad slang word to mimic the language of a US citizen.

9538397f-67a9-4518-8691-ca72ffad4ad1.label: text_chunk
9538397f-67a9-4518-8691-ca72ffad4ad1.text:
Thus, we model the presence of such words using a list of bad and sexual words from [11].

– Stance Cues :

 Stance detection has been studied in different contexts to detect the stance of a tweet reply with respect to a main tweet/thread [24]. Using this feature, we aim to detect the stance of the users regarding the different topics we extracted. To model the stance we use a set of stance lexicons employed in previous works [3,12].

fc8b6ecd-f4dc-480c-a9ad-00fe41a38e72.label: text_chunk
fc8b6ecd-f4dc-480c-a9ad-00fe41a38e72.text:
Concretely, we focus on the fol-
6 Anonymous et al.

lowing categories: belief, denial, doubt, fake, knowledge, negation, question, and report .

– Bias Cues :

 We rely on a set of lexicons to capture the bias in text.

6e463b30-3529-4f4a-a208-a2ab69112bf9.label: text_chunk
6e463b30-3529-4f4a-a208-a2ab69112bf9.text:
We model the presence of the words in one of the following cues categories: assertives verbs [17], bias [28], factive verbs [21], implicative verbs [20], hedges [18], report verbs [28]. A previous work has used these bias cues to identify bias in suspicious news posts in Twitter [31].

– LIWC :

 We use a set of linguistic categories from the LIWC linguistic dic- tionary [30].

0d8f2bed-83cd-4c75-a541-99d0af5ae4e8.label: text_chunk
0d8f2bed-83cd-4c75-a541-99d0af5ae4e8.text:
The used categories are: pronoun, anx, cogmech, insight, cause, discrep, tentat, certain, inhib, incl 9 .

– Morality :

 Cues based on the morality foundation theory [15] where words labeled in one of a set of categories: care, harm, fairness, cheating, loyalty, betrayal, authority, subversion, sanctity, and degradation .Given V i as the concatenation of the previous features vectors of a tweet i ,

7dc26b03-d5ba-4ef5-98f8-ddfa264b77ef.text: we represent each user’s tweets by considering the average and standard deviation of her tweets’ V 1 , 2 ,.N in each theme j independently and we concatenate them.

user x = T K j =1   P N j i =1 V ij N j s P N j i =1 ( V ij − V j ) N j   (1)

With this representation we aim at capturing the ”Flip-Flop” behavior of IRA trolls among the themes (see Section 5.4).

710dbdd2-dda1-4654-9e2c-3d8a72b245de.text:
4.2 Proﬁling IRA Accounts

As Twitter declared, although the IRA campaign was originated in Russia, it has been found that IRA trolls concealed their identity by tweeting in English. Furthermore, for any possibility of unmasking their identity, the majority of IRA trolls changed their location to other countries and the language of the Twitter interface they use.

17420d01-c105-407f-9d63-7c328cfea8db.label: text_chunk
17420d01-c105-407f-9d63-7c328cfea8db.text:
Thus, we propose the following features to identify these users using only their tweets text:

– Native Language Identification (NLI) :

 This feature was inspired by earlier works on identifying native language of essays writers [22]. We aim to detect IRA trolls by identifying their way of writing English tweets. As shown in [31], English tweets generated by non-English speakers have a different syntactic pattern .

5efc1862-6732-4796-bf63-9ea031c6587f.label: text_chunk
5efc1862-6732-4796-bf63-9ea031c6587f.text:
Thus, we use state-of-the-art NLI features to detect this unique pattern [8,23,14]; the feature set consists of bag of stopwords, Part- of-speech tags (POS), and syntactic dependency relations (DEPREL). We
9 Total pronouns, Anxiety, Cognitive processes, Insight, Causation, Discrepancy, Ten- tative, Certainty, Inhibition, and Inclusive respectively.

extract the POS and the DEPREL information using spaCy 10 , an off-the- shelf POS tagger 11 . We clean the tweets from the special characters and maintained dots, commas, and first-letter capitalization of words. We use regular expressions to convert a sequence of dots to a single dot, and similarly for sequence of characters.

b250b50e-f659-40fc-9f78-4c53309c643b.text:
– Stylistic :

 We extract a set of stylistic features following previous works in the authorship attribution domain [33,4,29], such as: the count of spe- cial characters, consecutive characters and letters 12 , URLs, hashtags, users’ mentions.

5c10f0cc-5668-413d-b954-dcd3b94f851d.label: text_chunk

5 Experiments and Analysis

5.1 Experimental Setup

We report precision, recall and F1 score. Given the substantial class imbalance in the dataset, we use the macro weighted version of the F1 metric. We tested several classifiers and Logistic Regression showed the best F1 macro value. We kept the default parameters values. We report results for 5-folds cross-validation.

8fbc068d-adcf-424a-81c7-146d858ce0c5.label: text_chunk
8fbc068d-adcf-424a-81c7-146d858ce0c5.text:
5.2 Baselines

In order to evaluate our feature set, we use Random Selection , Majority Class , and bag-of-words baselines. In the bag-of-words baseline, we aggregate all the tweets of a user into one document. A previous work [6] showed that IRA trolls were playing a hashtag game which is a popular word game played on Twitter, where users add a hashtag to their tweets and then answer an implied question [16].

203f954a-5c1c-4ca7-b9fd-8db899cf95cb.label: text_chunk
203f954a-5c1c-4ca7-b9fd-8db899cf95cb.text:
IRA trolls used this game in a similar way but focusing more on offending or attacking others; an example from IRA tweets: ” #OffendEveryoneIn4Words un- documented immigrants are ILLEGALS ”. Thus, we use as a baseline Tweet2vec [10] which is a a character-based Bidirectional Gated Recurrent neural network
10 https://spacy.io/models

0bc519ff-c3a6-49a9-891f-3eee4e019ea5.label: text_chunk
0bc519ff-c3a6-49a9-891f-3eee4e019ea5.text: 11 We tested TweetNLP [27], the state-of-the-art POS tagger on tweets, but spaCy produced higher overall results.12 We considered 2 or more consecutive characters, and 3 or more consecutive letters.8 Anonymous et al.reads tweets and predicts their hashtags. We aim to assess if the tweets hashtags can help identifying the IRA tweets.

7cde9940-2bef-4f11-9b1b-ebf033f7b7df.label: text_chunk
7cde9940-2bef-4f11-9b1b-ebf033f7b7df.text:
The model reads the tweets in a form of character one-hot encodings and uses them for training with their hashtags as labels.

 To train the model, we use our collected dataset which consists of ∼ 3.7M tweets 13 .

1f6f4efb-4ca8-46e4-9e82-f7319eb447d1.label: text_chunk
1f6f4efb-4ca8-46e4-9e82-f7319eb447d1.text: To represent the tweets in this baseline, we use the decoded embedding produced by the model and we feed them to the Logistic Regression classifier.IRA dataset provided by Twitter contains less information about the ac- counts details, and they limited to: profile description, account creation date, number of followers and followees, location, and account language.

118b5ce5-21fa-4a5c-adb9-6c2d86bcbfb7.label: text_chunk
118b5ce5-21fa-4a5c-adb9-6c2d86bcbfb7.text:
Therefore, as another baseline we use the number of followers and followees to assess their identification ability (we will mention them as Network Features in the rest of the paper).

5.3 Results

Table 2 presents the classification results showing the performance of each feature set independently. Generally, we can see that the thematic information improves the performance of the proposed features clearly (RQ1), and with the largest amount in the Emotions features (see − themes and + themes columns).

54a766ab-2e0f-46cf-b39f-d813ebeed4da.label: text_chunk
54a766ab-2e0f-46cf-b39f-d813ebeed4da.text:
This result emphasizes the importance of the thematic information.

 Also, we see that the emotions performance increases with the largest amount considering F1 macro value; this motivates us to analyze the emotions in IRA tweets (see the following section).The result of the NLI feature in the table is interesting; we are able to detect IRA trolls from their writing style with a F1 macro value of 0.91.

6719bf04-c1ec-4505-96b2-7a66fe49e32c.label: text_chunk
6719bf04-c1ec-4505-96b2-7a66fe49e32c.text: Considering the results in Table 2, we can notice that we are able to detect the IRA trolls effectively using only textual features (RQ2).Finally, the baselines results show us that the Network features do not per- form well. A previous work [32] showed that IRA trolls tend to follow a lot of users, and nudging other users to follow them (e.g.

Finally, similar to the Network features, the Tweet2vec baseline performs poorly. This indicates that, although IRA trolls used the hashtag game extensively in their tweets, the Tweet2vec baseline is not able to identify them.

The analysis shows that the themes that were used to attack immigrants ( Black People and Islam and War ) have the fear emotion in their top two emotions.

Some of the misclassified trolls mention only external links without a clear textual content. This kind of trolls needs a second step to investigate the content of the external links.

6 Conclusion

In this work, we present a textual approach to detect social media trolls, namely IRA accounts. Due to the anonymity characteristic that social media provide to users, these kinds of suspicious behavioural accounts have started to appear. We built a new machine learning model based on theme-based and profiling features that in cross-validation evaluation achieved a F1 macro value of 0.94.

103b4fda-d246-4dd3-a249-0a5fe8f86a62.label: text_chunk
103b4fda-d246-4dd3-a249-0a5fe8f86a62.text:
We applied a topic modeling algorithm to go behind the superficial textual information of the tweets.

 Our experiments showed that the extracted themes boosted the per- formance of the proposed model when coupled with other surface text features. In addition, we proposed NLI features to identify IRA trolls from their writing style, which showed to be very effective.

9be86aad-4155-41c4-9f8e-502585c63edb.label: text_chunk
9be86aad-4155-41c4-9f8e-502585c63edb.text:
Finally, for a better understanding we analyzed the IRA accounts from emotional and linguistic perspectives.Through the manually checking of IRA accounts, we noticed that frequently irony was employed.

 As a future work, it would be interesting to identify these accounts by integrating an irony detection module.TexTrolls: Identifying Russian Trolls on Twitter from a Textual Perspective 13
